task_completion.description: Create a primary mask for the skull bone
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius interface showing a mask corresponding to the skull bone in the mask list or highlighted in the 2D slice views.

task_completion.description: Create a core mask by eroding the primary bone mask
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius interface showing a second mask in the mask list, representing the eroded inner core of the skull.

task_completion.description: Create a hollow shell mask via boolean subtraction
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius interface showing a third mask in the mask list (representing the hollow shell, typically created by subtracting the core from the bone mask).

task_completion.description: Generate a 3D surface from the hollow shell mask
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D viewer in InVesalius displaying the generated 3D surface model of the hollow skull shell.

task_completion.description: Export the 3D surface as an STL file
task_completion.visual_evidence: An export dialog or file manager showing the 3D surface being saved as 'hollow_skull.stl' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.
A save dialog or file manager showing the project being saved as 'hollow_project.inv3' in the '/home/ga/Documents/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the InVesalius GUI to perform the operations
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through InVesalius menus, mask tools, and boolean operations rather than using terminal scripts.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to fake output files
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like 'touch hollow_skull.stl' or 'touch hollow_project.inv3'.

integrity.description: The 3D model and masks correspond to the loaded CT Cranium DICOM scan
integrity.visual_evidence: The 2D slices and 3D viewer show a human skull, consistent with the provided medical imaging data.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected outputs are an STL file at /home/ga/Documents/hollow_skull.stl and an InVesalius project at /home/ga/Documents/hollow_project.inv3. The project must contain at least 3 masks (original bone, eroded core, and hollow shell). The hollow shell is typically created via boolean subtraction of the core from the bone mask.
